input.title: Bubble Sort instead of std::sort

output.technical: The code implements Bubble Sort, which has an average and worst-case time complexity of O(N^2). `std::sort` from the `<algorithm>` library uses Introsort, which runs in O(N log N).
output.beginner: You wrote your own sorting system that is very slow for large lists. C++ has a built-in sort that is incredibly fast.
output.analogy: It's like alphabetizing a library by swapping adjacent books one by one, instead of organizing them into categories first.
output.prevention: Never write your own sort function unless it's an educational exercise. Always use `std::sort`.

output.beginner: You wrote your own sorting system that is very slow for large lists. C++ has a built-in sort that is incredibly fast.
output.analogy: It's like alphabetizing a library by swapping adjacent books one by one, instead of organizing them into categories first.
output.prevention: Never write your own sort function unless it's an educational exercise. Always use `std::sort`.
